title: cornus macrophylla var. macrophylla
intro: Cornus macrophylla var. macrophylla, also known as the large-leaved dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves and showy white or pinkish flowers in the spring. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds found in its berries, roots, and leaves. The health benefits of Cornus macrophylla var. macrophylla include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
